title: selaginella ingens
intro: Selaginella ingens, also known as the "giant spikemoss" or "prince of Wales fern", is a species of plant in the genus Selaginella, which belongs to the family Selaginellaceae. It is a perennial plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine. The plant has been found to possess antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help to treat various health conditions, such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. Selaginella ingens can be prepared into herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and has been used to promote overall health and well-being, including reducing stress and improving the immune system.
